news.title: Mumbai: No medical bail to Mid-City Heights' partner in ₹56 crore real estate fraud
news.para: A Mumbai special court has denied medical bail to businessman Amarjeet Shukla, accused in a Rs 55.71 crore real estate fraud. The court found his medical condition, while needing treatment, did not constitute a life-threatening emergency justifying release from custody. Shukla sought bail for surgery, but the court deemed state facilities adequate.

news.title: Mumbai: In a first, SRA starts freezing builders’ sale flats to secure tenants’ rent
news.para: The Slum Rehabilitation Authority (SRA) is now freezing sale flats equivalent to three years' rent to ensure eligible slum dwellers receive their payments and new homes. This measure aims to prevent developers from defaulting on transit rent obligations. The SRA is also pushing for faster project completion and has collected over Rs 1,000 crore in rent.

news.title: Bombay HC backs housing society, allows new builder after 13-year delay
news.para: The Bombay High Court has dismissed a builder's plea to stop a Wadala housing society from appointing a new developer for redeveloping 46 buildings. The court emphasized that tenants, residing in structures built in 1957, cannot wait indefinitely for safer homes. The builder's challenge cited project delays, but the court prioritized the members' urgent need for redevelopment.

news.title: Compliance to safety norms a must for buildings: Karnataka HC
news.para: The Karnataka High Court has ruled that high-rise buildings, even those with compact apartments, cannot be built if they endanger public safety. This decision came as a division bench dismissed a real estate developer's appeal regarding fire safety setbacks. The court emphasized that public safety is paramount.

news.title: Goa RERA directs Aansav Realty to hand over flat to buyer, pay ₹15 lakh penalty
news.para: Goa RERA has ordered Aansav Realty to hand over a flat to a buyer within 60 days, after finding the developer unlawfully cancelled her allotment and delayed possession by over seven years. The authority also imposed a Rs 10 lakh penalty and Rs 5 lakh costs on the developer for violating RERA provisions.

news.title: Over 140 to get ₹50 lakh house repair aid in Ludhiana’s Ward 72
news.para: Ludhiana residents from weaker sections will receive financial aid for home repairs. The Rangla Punjab scheme will help 144 beneficiaries fix dilapidated roofs and other damages. Each recipient gets Rs 35,000. This initiative is part of a larger development plan for urban areas. Bank details are being collected for prompt payments.

news.title: Navi Mumbai civic body flags 500 buildings as unsafe, issues vacate notices
news.para: Navi Mumbai Municipal Corporation has declared 500 buildings dangerous, issuing notices for residents to vacate. This action follows a survey and aligns with high court orders. The civic body emphasizes mandatory structural audits for older buildings and requires permission for repairs, holding societies and flat owners accountable for accidents.

news.title: Bengaluru property owners can now download e-khatas online
news.para: Bengaluru property owners can now download e-khatas online via the GBA's e-Aasthi platform, eliminating office visits.  This digital initiative, accessible with a SAS property tax ID, streamlines documentation for tax payments, loans, and transactions.  While provisional, it aims to cut red tape and corruption, with full legal validity requiring further steps like Aadhaar KYC and deed updates.

news.title: Delhi police verifies 12,860 tenants so far this year
news.para: Delhi Police has registered nearly 900 cases this year against individuals for violating mandatory tenant verification, with over 12,860 forms filled. This process, crucial for preventive policing, helps maintain updated records of city residents. Landlords can complete verification online or offline at police stations.

news.title: More demolitions ahead in Gurugram, this time in HSVP sectors
news.para: Haryana Shahari Vikas Pradhikaran (HSVP) is launching a large-scale anti-encroachment drive across 44 sectors throughout May and June. This operation aims to clear public infrastructure like roads and utilities of unauthorized structures, following a similar drive by the DTCP. The move is crucial for ensuring smooth pedestrian movement, emergency access, and road safety.

news.title: At ₹2,000 crore, property tax dues are nearly one-third of Nagpur civic body's budget
news.para: Nagpur Municipal Corporation faces a staggering Rs1,919 crore in unpaid property tax, with 4.54 lakh properties in default, some for over 25 years. This systemic failure, with a significant portion owed by long-term defaulters and open plots, severely impacts the civic body's finances and service delivery capabilities.

news.title: Uttar Pradesh expands housing scheme to cover all remaining scheduled tribes
news.para: Uttar Pradesh has expanded its Mukhyamantri Awas Yojana-Gramin to cover all remaining Scheduled Tribes, aiming for universal housing. This decision follows the near saturation of earlier target groups and aims to fill a housing shortfall of approximately 6,000 units. The move will enable numerous tribal communities to receive permanent housing benefits.

news.title: Karnataka HC cites public safety, bins builder’s appeal over fire-safety setback violations
news.para: High-rise buildings that endanger public safety will not be permitted to stand. The High Court dismissed an appeal by Vishnu Sri Builders and Developers. The court found that a builder failed to maintain required setbacks. This deficiency prevents fire engines from accessing the property. The ruling prioritizes public safety over private interests.

news.title: Maharashtra digitises housing society registration; documents slashed from 29 to 19
news.para: Maharashtra's cooperation department has launched a fully online system on the Aaple Sarkar Portal to digitize cooperative housing society registrations. This reform significantly reduces paperwork and streamlines the process, aiming to clear a backlog of unregistered buildings and empower residents by eliminating middlemen and simplifying procedures.

news.title: Telangana government preparing guidelines for revision of land values
news.para: The registration department is preparing to revise land values for property registrations, aiming to bridge the gap between government rates and market prices. This move, expected to increase revenue by ₹2,000 crore, could see hikes of 30-50% in some areas, while potentially reducing rates in others.

news.title: India Cements' profit jumps fourfold to ₹59 crore in Q4 FY26
news.para: India Cements Ltd. witnessed a significant surge in its March quarter net profit, soaring to Rs 59.5 crore from Rs 14.67 crore a year ago. This impressive growth was fueled by a substantial 18% increase in domestic sales volume and improved sales realization.

news.title: ED raids against Raheja Developers in home buyers 'fraud' case
news.para: The Enforcement Directorate has conducted fresh searches against Raheja Developers in Delhi-NCR as part of a money laundering probe linked to alleged fraud with home buyers. The investigation focuses on the non-delivery of flats in the 'Raheja Revanta' project. The company denies wrongdoing, citing project delays due to a lack of essential government infrastructure.

news.title: Andhra Pradesh high court orders demolition of illegal Thotlakonda resort
news.para: The Andhra Pradesh High Court has ordered the demolition of illegal structures at a beach resort near the Thotlakonda Buddhist monument. The resort, built on land leased by APTDC and allegedly subleased for unauthorized expansion including permanent concrete structures, violated Coastal Regulation Zone norms. The court directed the GVMC to demolish the constructions within three weeks.

news.title: Bengaluru: Alleged norm violations cloud Shankarapuram building project
news.para: According to reports, construction began in February, and it was noticed that concrete pillars were erected by damaging and blocking a nearby drain, raising concerns about public infrastructure

news.title: East Khasi Hills bans illegal sand mining from river beds
news.para: In a bold move to protect the environment, the East Khasi Hills district has enacted a ban on illegal mining, hillside quarrying, and unauthorized sand extraction from riverbeds. This decisive action comes in response to the significant ecological damage observed near the Wahniangleng river, where vital water resources and aquatic habitats have suffered.
